question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I want to run one planning thread with explicit decision boundaries before our next check-in.

assessment.note: She names a concrete next focus—running one planning thread with explicit decision boundaries—which shows some prioritization and initiative, though it also reinforces the broader decision-boundary gap already surfaced.

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
If I have explicit decision boundaries for this quarter, I can lead much more confidently.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.
I want to run one planning thread with explicit decision boundaries before our next check-in.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
Being remote makes it harder to read context that others seem to pick up naturally.
I want to drive alignment, but I do not want to create noise by over-communicating.
I still need a clearer picture of how roadmap tradeoffs are finalized here.
A consistent partner check-in cadence would help me validate assumptions earlier.
